name: Puff Pastry
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the flour and salt in a bowl.
2. Cut 1 cup of butter into small pieces and add to the flour mixture. Make a well in the center and gradually add the water, stirring until combined.
3. Turn the dough out onto a clean working surface (preferably marble) and knead for about 10 minutes, adding only a little flour if necessary.
4. Chill the dough for half an hour.
5. Meanwhile, prepare the butter by mixing it with 2 tablespoons of flour to combine.
6. Form the butter into an even 4-inch square rectangle and chill for 20 minutes.
7. Roll out the dough to a thickness of about 1/2 inch. Place the butter rectangle in the center and fold the sides over the butter, covering it slightly and overlapping. Press the edges with a rolling pin.
8. Roll out the dough again to a thickness of about 1 inch. Fold into thirds like a letter. Roll out once more and repeat the folding process.
9. Chill the rolled-out dough for half an hour wrapped in plastic film and covered with a damp cloth.
10. Repeat steps 7-9 three times, resulting in six total rolls and folds.
11. The puff pastry can be frozen for up to 6 months or stored in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
